summary: Backend-only testing of the new Midtrans Snap + mock-email integration on top of the InScale Digital booking app. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_midtrans_integration.py (13 new tests) covering: booking creation now returns snap_token/midtrans_client_key/midtrans_is_production/expires_at/payment_status; public GET /api/bookings/{id} hides Midtrans secrets; webhook signature validation (403 on bad sig); state transitions on settlement/cancel/expire; idempotency of settlement; email_logs persistence (MOCK provider) for created+confirmation+cancelled; admin /api/admin/stats now includes 'paid' and revenue_idr is sum of amount_idr for paid bookings; admin /api/admin/bookings projection excludes snap_token/midtrans_transaction_id/midtrans_redirect_url and serialises expires_at as ISO; PATCH confirmed/completed unsets expires_at (TTL); TTL index on bookings.expires_at present. All 42/42 tests pass (29 iteration-1 regression + 13 new).

minor.issue: On Midtrans failure the held booking row is deleted (cleanup) and a 502 is returned — but the prior MOCK email of kind='created' is sent BEFORE the Midtrans call ONLY if Midtrans succeeds. Today it's correctly ordered (Midtrans call first, then send_booking_email). However the rollback path doesn't write a 'failed-to-create' email_log, which is fine but worth noting if observability is later required.

On 'pending' transaction_status the code re-writes status/payment_status (=current values + 'pending'), but does NOT extend or refresh expires_at. Mostly harmless because Midtrans Snap also has its own expiry. Could optionally bump expires_at on intermediate states.

tested_flows_backend: POST /api/bookings -> 200 with snap_token (>10 chars), midtrans_client_key matches .env, midtrans_is_production=true, expires_at within +25..35 min, payment_status='pending', status='pending'
GET /api/bookings/{id} -> 200 with payment_status, amount_idr=500000, NO snap_token, NO midtrans_transaction_id, expires_at=ISO string

POST /api/payment/notification settlement (valid sha512 sig) -> booking becomes payment_status='paid', status='confirmed', paid_at set, expires_at unset; email_logs has both 'created' and 'confirmation' for booking_id; second identical settlement is idempotent (returns ok + note='already-paid')
POST /api/payment/notification cancel/expire -> payment_status='failed', status='cancelled', email_logs has 'cancelled'
GET /api/admin/stats -> includes paid + revenue_idr (>=500000 after a single settled booking of digital-consulting)
GET /api/admin/bookings -> no _id, no snap_token, no midtrans_transaction_id, no midtrans_redirect_url; expires_at (when present) is ISO string
PATCH /api/admin/bookings/{id} status=confirmed -> 200, response.expires_at is null/empty, persisted GET also has no expires_at
Mongo bookings collection has a TTL index on expires_at with expireAfterSeconds present
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~790 lines — still manageable but approaching the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers (auth.py, bookings.py, admin.py, midtrans.py) plus a services/email.py + services/midtrans.py layer in the next iteration.
Mock email service writes via global `db` from inside send_booking_email — fine for now, but when swapping to Resend it will be cleaner to inject the collection.

Webhook handler does not store the incoming Midtrans payload (only a flat midtrans_last_status/payment_type). For future dispute/audit purposes consider persisting the raw notification body in a payment_events collection.

Webhook returns {'ok': true} even when the booking cannot be found (logged but 200). Midtrans expects 200 for success, but a 200 hides genuine 'order_id mismatch' bugs. Consider returning 404 or a distinct logged metric.
`midtransclient.Snap` is constructed per request inside create_booking. Cheap, but a module-level singleton would save ~5ms and make config-change behaviour explicit.

Production keys (MIDTRANS_SERVER_KEY=Mid-server-...) are committed in /app/backend/.env. This is correct for the dev container per user instruction, but flag this clearly in deployment docs — these are LIVE production credentials.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@inscaledigital.id / AdminInscale#2026 (verified). Midtrans server key read from /app/backend/.env at test time to forge sha512 webhook signatures.
seed_data_creation: All test data is prefixed TEST_ and is auto-cleaned by a module-scoped autouse fixture (deletes bookings, email_logs, login_attempts after the suite). Live Midtrans Snap transactions ARE created against production (one per booking in tests) — they auto-expire in 30 min, no payment is captured.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two test files exist: test_inscale_api.py (regression, 29 tests) and test_midtrans_integration.py (Midtrans, 13 tests). They share fixtures via a relative import — tests must be run from /app with `pytest /app/backend/tests/`. Webhook signatures are sha512(order_id+status_code+gross_amount+SERVER_KEY) where SERVER_KEY is read from /app/backend/.env. NOTE: each create-booking test creates a real Snap transaction at https://app.midtrans.com — the merchant currently shows 'No payment channels available' but the token call still succeeds. Do NOT call the real Midtrans webhook publicly; only POST locally to /api/payment/notification with forged signatures. TTL index on expires_at exists; admin PATCH to confirmed/completed correctly $unsets it.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Integration is working end-to-end against live Midtrans production keys.
